records.ety: [Kid a child + Prov. E. nap to seize, to grasp. Cf. Knab, Knap, Nab.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Kidnaped (kd"npt`) or Kidnapped; p. pr. & vb. n. Kidnaping or Kidnapping.]

defs: To take (any one) by force or fear, and against one's will, with intent to carry to another place.
quotes: You may reason or expostulate with the parents, but never attempt to kidnap their children, and to make proselytes of them.
notes: ☞ Originally used only of stealing children, but now extended in application to any human being, involuntarily abducted.
